question_title: What will be the abstention rate in the first round of the 2026 French municipal elections?
summary: French municipal first-round abstention in 2026 should revert massively from the 2020 COVID anomaly (55.3%) but continue the long-term upward trend from 2014 (36.5%). Historical trend models extrapolate to 38-42%, polling shows 60% "certain to vote" (suggesting ~40% abstention), and political disaffection since the 2024 dissolution adds upward pressure. My scenario-based Monte Carlo simulation yields a median of ~42%, with the main uncertainty being whether the outcome lands in the "normal trend reversion" zone (~38-41%) or the "moderate disaffection" zone (~42-45%). Cross-checks against other recent French elections (2024 European at 48.5%, 2024 snap legislative at 33.3%) and polling data all support a central range of 39-44%.

factors.description: Pre-COVID upward trend extrapolation from 2014 (36.5%) suggests ~38-42% for 2026, with decelerating growth rate of ~1.4pp per 6-year cycle
2020 COVID anomaly (55.3%) will not repeat - no pandemic, seniors can return to polls. Expert Frédéric Dabi (Ifop) confirms 'certainly better than 2020'
Political disaffection since 2024 dissolution and general distrust of national politics pushes abstention above pre-COVID trend
Polling: 60% 'certain to vote' nationally (IFOP Nov 2025), 69% interested in campaign (OpinionWay Jan 2026) - suggests decent mobilization
Competitive high-profile races in Paris, Marseille, Lyon, Bordeaux with new city-wide voting system generate interest and turnout
New proportional list system for all communes may reduce personal connection in small towns, potentially slightly raising abstention
Post-COVID structural shift in voting habits visible in other elections (2021 regionals 66.7%, 2022 legislative 52.5%) suggests some lasting effect

reasoning:
Let me fetch the specific articles with polling/abstention data and the historical INSEE data.Let me search for more specific polling data on voter intention for the 2026 elections and fetch the articles with relevant predictions.Now let me save my research findings and check prediction markets.Now let me run quantitative analysis and check prediction markets.Now let me write the meta-reflection and finalize.Based on my analysis, here's the summary of my forecast:

**Historical trend**: Municipal election abstention has risen steadily from 21.6% (1983) to 36.5% (2014), with a decelerating rate of ~1.4pp/cycle by 2014. The 2020 result (55.3%) was a COVID anomaly. Trend extrapolation to 2026 gives ~38-42%.

**Key factors**:
- **No COVID**: Seniors return, fear removed → strong downward pressure from 2020 levels
- **Political disaffection**: Post-2024 dissolution, low trust in national politics → upward pressure above pre-COVID trend
- **Competitive races**: Paris, Marseille, Lyon, Bordeaux all contested → supports turnout
- **Polling**: 60% "certain to vote" (IFOP Nov 2025), 69% interested (OpinionWay Jan 2026) → suggests ~38-42% abstention range
- **Structural voting changes**: New proportional list system for all communes → uncertain effect
- **Expert view** (Ifop's Dabi): "Certainly better than 2020, but hard to project"

**Cross-checks converge**: European election gap method → 38-41%; polling → ~34-40%; trend models → 38-43%.

My Monte Carlo simulation (100k runs, 5 weighted scenarios) produces a median of ~42%, reflecting the pre-COVID trend plus modest uplift from political disaffection.
